channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Two Ohio women arrested after 8-year-old's body found hidden in attic
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- Two women wanted in connection with the death of an 8-year-old Columbus boy turned themselves in Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Mark Feuerborn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon surrenders to federal prison to serve 4-month sentence on contempt charges
item.description: DANBURY, Conn. (AP) — Longtime Trump ally Steve Bannon was taken into custody Monday after surrendering at a federal prison to begin a four-month sentence on contempt charges for defying a subpoena in the congressional investigation into the U.S. Capitol attack.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stingray that got pregnant despite no male companion has died, aquarium says
item.description: HENDERSONVILLE, N.C. (AP) — A stingray that got pregnant at a North Carolina aquarium this winter despite not having shared a tank with a male of her species for many years has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ukraine is releasing thousands of prisoners so they can join the fight against Russia
item.description: DNIPROPETROVSK REGION, Ukraine (AP) — At a rural penal colony in southeast Ukraine, several convicts stand assembled under barbed wire to hear an army recruiter offer them a shot at parole. In return, they must join the grueling fight against Russia.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, EVGENIY MALOLETKA and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Missing Georgia firefighters found dead in baffling circumstances: police
item.description: In a Georgia mother's worst nightmare, the bodies of her missing son and his ex-girlfriend were found Sunday in Tennessee after a desperate weekslong search.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans sue Attorney General Garland over access to Biden special counsel interview audio
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans on Monday filed a lawsuit against Attorney General Merrick Garland for the audio recording of President Joe Biden's interview with a special counsel in his classified documents case, asking the courts to enforce their subpoena and reject the White House's effort t
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Court allows key part of Biden student loan relief plan to resume
item.description: A federal appeals court on Sunday ruled that a key part of President Joe Biden’s student loan forgiveness plan can resume after an injunction was placed on the initiative.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas, KRON4

item.title: Ohio college student dies in Rome while studying abroad
item.description: Ohio college student Connor DiNapoli, a marketing major at the University of Dayton, died unexpectedly last week in Rome while studying abroad for the summer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Model and 'Guiding Light' actor Renauld White dies at 80
item.description: Renauld White, a pioneering figure in the modeling industry who appeared on “Guiding Light” in the 1980s, died Wednesday, his close friend revealed. He was 80 years old.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have broad immunity, dimming chance of a pre-election Trump trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday ruled for the first time that former presidents have some immunity from prosecution, extending the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss and all but ending prospec
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jurors in Karen Read trial again say they are deadlocked. Judge tells them to continue deliberating
item.description: DEDHAM, Mass. (AP) — A judge ordered the jury in the Karen Read murder trial to continue deliberating Monday after they said for a second time that they were deadlocked. Jurors had first told the judge on Friday that they were deadlocked, only for her to ask them to continue deliberating.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Republicans who want to be Trump's VP were once harsh critics with key policy differences
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s hard to refer to someone as “Hitler” and end up in their good graces, let alone potentially become the person they choose to help lead the country. But Ohio Sen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: AH-WOOOO!!!🐺🌊 Great Wolf Lodge is opening in the Greater Houston area; Here's how YOU can be part of the pack
item.description: Great Wolf Lodge Texas Gulf Coast is opening SOON, and they're looking to hire 600+ new Pack Members.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Ballard, KPRC 2

item.title: Cup Noodles releases new s'mores flavor for summer 2024
item.description: (WJET/WFXP)-- Have you ever been enjoying a nice s'more by the campfire and thought to yourself, "What if it came as a cup of ramen noodles?" Probably not, but now you can with a new release from Cup Noodles.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, Fox 5

item.title: Jurors in Karen Read trial again say they are deadlocked. Judge tells them to continue deliberating
item.description: A judge ordered the jury in the Karen Read murder trial to continue deliberating Monday after they said for a second time that they were deadlocked.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Can Trump be prosecuted in Jan. 6, election interference cases?
item.description: The ruling means that Jack Smith, who brought the case against Trump, will likely have to refigure his arguments under the new ruling.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Jury deadlocked in Karen Read murder trial, judge pushes once more for verdict
item.description: Years of tensions, mudslinging and conspiracies remains deadlocked because of "deeply held convictions" in murder trial of Karen Read, the jury's note says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman attacked in shocking Pride parade beating caught on video
item.description: New York City police arrested 15 after videos of Pride parade chaos show multiple assaults and other disorderly conduct in Washington Square Park Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boeing agrees to acquire Spirit AeroSystem
item.description: (WJET/WFXP) — Boeing announced on Monday that they have agreed to a definitive merger agreement to acquire Spirit AreoSystems. "We believe this deal is in the best interest of the flying public, our airline customers, the employees of Spirit and Boeing, our shareholders and the country more broadly.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have some immunity, sends Trump case back to lower court
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: After 32 years as a progressive voice for LGBTQ Jews, Rabbi Sharon Kleinbaum heads into retirement
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For more than three decades, Rabbi Sharon Kleinbaum has led the nation’s largest LGBTQ+ synagogue through the myriad ups and downs of the modern gay-rights movement — through the AIDS crisis, the murder of Matthew Shepard, the historic civil-rights advances that included marriage equ
item.dc:creator: KEN SWEET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The brutal killing of a Detroit man in 1982 inspires decades of Asian American activism nationwide
item.description: Two white autoworkers bludgeoned 27-year-old Chinese American Vincent Chin to death with a baseball bat during his bachelor party in Detroit in 1982, but his loved ones' cries for justice fell on deaf ears.
item.dc:creator: RYAN DOAN-NGUYEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps on hold efforts in Texas and Florida to regulate social media platforms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday kept a hold on efforts in Texas and Florida to limit how Facebook, TikTok, X, YouTube and other social media platforms regulate content posted by their users.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SCOTUS sends Trump immunity case back to lower court but issues ruling on presidential immunity
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday extended the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss, all but ending prospects the former president could be tried before the November election.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6.4M cars have defective airbags a decade after initial recall
item.description: More than 6 million cars on United States roads have a dangerous, potentially deadly, piece of equipment inside.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for a North Dakota truck stop in a new blow to federal regulations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court opened the door Monday to new, broad challenges to regulations long after they take effect, the third blow in a week to federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michael J. Fox rocks out with Coldplay at Glastonbury
item.description: Fans at Glastonbury Festival were surprised by a performance by Marty McFly. On Saturday, Michael J. Fox joined Coldplay onstage during their set at the United Kingdom festival. The band's frontman Chris Martin welcomed the actor to the front of the stage in front of 100,000 fans.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have some immunity, sends Trump case back to lower court
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats stick with Biden after poor debate performance
item.description: Following CNN’s presidential debate Thursday Democrats in Congress say they're sticking by President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dangerous Hurricane Beryl restrengthens to Category 4 off Grenada
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Beryl has restrengthened into an extremely dangerous Category 4 hurricane. The National Hurricane Center in Miami said the storm was located about 70 miles (125 kilometers) east of Grenada on Monday morning and had maximum sustained winds of 130 mph (215 kph.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Inside VidCon 2024: Here's the secret to becoming a social media star
item.description: VidCon is a place where creators, their fans, brands and merch collide. "Hollywood is no longer just Hollywood," explains Sarah Tortoreti, VidCon's SVP of Marketing and Communications.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: US Supreme Court Latest: Court sends Trump’s immunity case back to lower court
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court justices have sent Donald Trump’s immunity case back to a lower court in Washington, dimming the prospect of a pre-election trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Voters kick all the Republican women out of the South Carolina Senate
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — The only three Republican women in the South Carolina Senate took on their party and stopped a total abortion ban from passing in their state last year. In return, they lost their jobs. Voters removed Sens.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court decision on Trump immunity expected Monday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court's final day of the term will be Monday, when it issues a critical decision on whether former President Donald Trump has immunity from prosecution for his actions related to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing mom, 2 daughters may have been 'coerced to travel to Mexico': Kansas authorities
item.description: Investigators believe all three may have been coerced into traveling to "a religious rehabilitation facility" in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Carina Branson, WGN 9

item.title: Family demands accountability for NY police killing of 13-year-old boy. Police said he aimed BB gun
item.description: Relatives of a 13-year-old boy who was shot and killed by police in central New York are demanding justice.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Exotic mammal native to Central and South America spotted at Washington rest stop
item.description: Also referred to as “honey bears," the species is usually found in tropical rainforests from southern Mexico to Brazil.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, WGN 9
